name: 约翰·塞巴斯蒂安·巴赫
brief: 巴赫出生于德国图林根州的艾森纳赫，来自一个世代从事音乐的家族。他一生大部分时间在教堂和宫廷担任管风琴师与乐长，生前并未享有今日的盛名。巴赫在莱比锡圣托马斯教堂度过了人生最后的27年，创作了大量宗教与世俗作品。他去世后几近被遗忘，直到19世纪门德尔松重新上演《马太受难曲》，巴赫才被世人重新发现，并被尊为「音乐之父」。
briefEn: Bach was born in Eisenach, Thuringia, into a family steeped in music for generations. He spent most of his life serving as organist and music director at churches and courts, and enjoyed nothing like his present-day fame during his lifetime. Bach spent his final 27 years at the Thomaskirche in Leipzig, producing an enormous body of sacred and secular works. After his death he was largely forgotten, until Mendelssohn's landmark revival of the St Matthew Passion in the 19th century restored him to the world's attention and earned him the title 'Father of Music.'
contribution: 巴赫将巴洛克时期的对位法（Counterpoint）发展到了登峰造极的境界。他的《平均律键盘曲集》系统性地展示了所有24个大小调的可能性，为后世的调性体系奠定了基石。他将意大利协奏风格、法国舞曲传统与德国对位技法融为一体，创造出一种综合性的音乐语言，深刻影响了此后三个世纪的西方音乐。
contributionEn: Bach brought Baroque counterpoint to its absolute pinnacle. His Well-Tempered Clavier systematically demonstrated the possibilities of all 24 major and minor keys, laying the cornerstone of the tonal system for centuries to come. By synthesizing the Italian concerto style, French dance traditions, and German contrapuntal craft, he forged a comprehensive musical language that profoundly shaped three centuries of Western music.
style: 巴赫的音乐以严密的对位结构著称，赋格与卡农是他最擅长的形式。他的作品兼具数学般的精确与深沉的情感力量——复杂的声部交织中蕴含着深邃的宗教虔诚与人文关怀。他的和声语汇极其丰富，大量使用半音化进行和意想不到的转调，赋予音乐一种超越时代的现代感。
styleEn: Bach's music is defined by its rigorous contrapuntal architecture, with fugue and canon as his supreme forms. His works unite mathematical precision with profound emotional depth — within the intricate interplay of voices lies a deep religious devotion and humane compassion. His harmonic vocabulary is extraordinarily rich, employing extensive chromaticism and unexpected modulations that lend the music a strikingly modern quality far ahead of its time.

famousScores.name: 《马太受难曲》 BWV 244
famousScores.desc: 巴洛克声乐巨作，以耶稣受难为题材的大型清唱剧
famousScores.descEn: A monumental Baroque vocal work — a large-scale oratorio depicting the Passion of Christ
famousScores.detail:
《马太受难曲》创作于1727年前后，为莱比锡圣托马斯教堂的受难周礼拜而作。全曲分为两大部分，以马太福音第26至27章为文本蓝本，巴赫将圣经叙事、咏叹调与众赞歌三种体裁有机交织，构筑起一座宏伟的音乐大教堂。乐曲需要双合唱团、双管弦乐团以及双管风琴同时演奏，堪称巴洛克时代规模最为壮观的声乐作品。

最令人动容的历史故事发生在1829年3月11日。彼时巴赫辞世已近80年，年仅20岁的门德尔松在柏林指挥重演了这部作品——这是该曲自1729年首演后首次公开演出。据说排练时许多歌手不相信有人能写出如此伟大的音乐，门德尔松的老师泽尔特甚至哭泣失声。当晚的演出震惊了整个柏林，黑格尔、海涅等文化名人都在场，门德尔松用一场音乐会重新唤醒了一位沉睡的巨人。

《马太受难曲》对音乐史的影响是决定性的。它的复兴直接催生了19世纪的「巴赫复兴运动」，使人们开始重新审视巴洛克时代的遗产。其合唱写作技法——尤其是双合唱团的空间对话——影响了勃拉姆斯的《德意志安魂曲》乃至马勒的交响曲。开篇合唱「来吧，女儿们，帮我哀悼」以其层叠的对位声部和质朴的众赞歌旋律，成为西方声乐史上最令人心碎的音乐时刻之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
The St Matthew Passion was composed around 1727 for the Good Friday liturgy at the Thomaskirche in Leipzig. Drawing on chapters 26 and 27 of Matthew's Gospel, Bach interweaves biblical narration, arias, and chorales into a vast musical cathedral requiring double choir, double orchestra, and double continuo — the most ambitious vocal work of the Baroque era.

The work's most celebrated historical episode came on March 11, 1829, nearly eighty years after Bach's death. The twenty-year-old Mendelssohn conducted its first public revival in Berlin — the first documented performance since 1729. Singers reportedly doubted that any composer could have written something so magnificent; Mendelssohn's own teacher Zelter wept openly. Hegel and Heine were in the audience, and the evening electrified Berlin, launching the great Bach Revival of the 19th century.

The Passion's influence on music history is decisive. Its resurrection made composers and scholars reassess the entire Baroque legacy. The double-choir spatial dialogue influenced Brahms's German Requiem and even Mahler's symphonies. The opening chorus, 'Come, daughters, help me lament,' with its layered counterpoint and plain chorale melody rising above, remains one of the most heartbreaking moments in all of Western vocal music.

famousScores.name: 《哥德堡变奏曲》 BWV 988
famousScores.desc: 30段变奏构成的键盘百科全书，结构精妙无比
famousScores.descEn: An encyclopedic keyboard work of 30 variations, breathtaking in its structural ingenuity
famousScores.detail:
《哥德堡变奏曲》约创作于1741年，是巴赫《键盘练习曲集》第四部分。全曲由一首咏叹调（Aria）和30段变奏构成，所有变奏均建立在同一组低音声部和声进行之上，每三段为一组，每组的第三段固定是一首双声部卡农——从同度卡农开始，依次递进至九度卡农，形成精密的数学架构。最后再现开篇咏叹调，构成首尾呼应的圆弧。

关于这部作品的起源，流传着一个迷人的故事：据18世纪音乐传记作家福尔克尔记载，萨克森驻俄宫廷大使凯泽林伯爵患有严重失眠症，他雇用了年轻的键盘手约翰·哥德堡（Johann Gottlieb Goldberg，实为巴赫学生）在失眠之夜为他演奏。伯爵委托巴赫创作一套让人在深夜聆听也不感到乏味的变奏曲，并以装满金路易的茶杯作为报酬。尽管现代学者对此故事的真实性有所质疑，但「哥德堡」这个名字却因此流传至今。

格伦·古尔德于1955年录制的《哥德堡变奏曲》是20世纪最具影响力的古典音乐录音之一，他以快速、不带装饰音的清晰演奏颠覆了人们对这部作品的理解，使这位当时名不见经传的加拿大年轻人一夜成名。1981年，古尔德重录了此曲，那是他去世前最后一次正式录音，两版录音合在一起几乎是他整个音乐生涯的缩影。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Goldberg Variations, published around 1741 as the fourth part of Bach's Keyboard Practice, comprises an Aria and 30 variations, all built over the same bass-line harmonic progression. The variations are arranged in groups of three, with every third variation being a two-voice canon — beginning at the unison and ascending through each interval to the ninth — creating an intricate mathematical architecture. The work ends with a return of the opening Aria, completing a perfect arc.

A charming story of the work's origin was recorded by Bach's biographer Forkel: Count Keyserlingk, Saxon ambassador to the Russian court, suffered from severe insomnia and employed the young keyboardist Johann Gottlieb Goldberg (one of Bach's pupils) to play for him during sleepless nights. He supposedly commissioned Bach to write variations that could be played in the small hours without becoming tiresome, rewarding him with a goblet filled with gold louis. Modern scholars question the tale's accuracy, but 'Goldberg' has stuck as the work's immortal nickname.

Glenn Gould's 1955 recording of the Variations is one of the most influential classical recordings of the 20th century. His rapid, ornament-light clarity of touch overturned received wisdom about the piece and made the unknown young Canadian pianist famous overnight. Gould re-recorded the work in 1981 — his last major studio recording before his death — and together the two versions form an uncanny bookend to his entire artistic life.

famousScores.name: 《平均律键盘曲集》 BWV 846–893
famousScores.desc: 48首前奏曲与赋格，覆盖全部24个大小调
famousScores.descEn: 48 preludes and fugues traversing all 24 major and minor keys
famousScores.detail:
《平均律键盘曲集》分为两卷（1722年和1742年），每卷包含24首前奏曲与赋格，依次覆盖C大调至B小调的全部24个调性。第一卷扉页上巴赫亲笔写道：「献给渴望学习的青年，以及已经精通此道者的娱乐」。这部作品最初的实用目的是证明一种新的调律方法——「平均律调律」能够使所有调性均可自如演奏，而非只有少数「纯净」的调性。

在历史上，这套曲集被称为「键盘音乐的旧约圣经」——舒曼给出的比喻，而贝多芬的新约圣经则是他的三十二首钢琴奏鸣曲。据记载，莫扎特在访问莱比锡时专程向托马斯教堂的当时乐长求借巴赫的手稿，抄录研习后大为震动；肖邦每次演出前都要弹奏一遍《平均律》作为热身；大卫·赫尔丁更声称「如果所有音乐都消失了，只留下这套曲集，我也能重建整个西方音乐」。

《平均律键盘曲集》奠定了西方调性体系的实践基础，并成为此后所有作曲家学习和声与对位的最高教本。巴赫在其中展示了赋格的无限可能——声部从2至5声部不等，性格从轻盈嬉戏到庄严沉重各异。德彪西、肖斯塔科维奇等人都曾创作自己版本的前奏曲与赋格集，向巴赫这部伟大原作致敬。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Well-Tempered Clavier was compiled in two volumes (1722 and 1742), each containing 24 preludes and fugues moving through all 24 major and minor keys from C major to B minor. On the title page of the first book, Bach wrote in his own hand that it was intended 'for the use and profit of musical youth desirous of learning, and especially for the pastime of those already skilled in this study.' Its original practical aim was to demonstrate that a new tuning system could render all keys playable with equal fluency.

The collection has been called 'the Old Testament of keyboard music' — a description attributed to Schumann, who paired it with Beethoven's 32 piano sonatas as the 'New Testament.' Mozart reportedly copied out Bach's manuscripts on a visit to Leipzig, emerging profoundly shaken; Chopin is said to have played through the 48 before every concert as a warm-up ritual; and the scholar David Hellding once claimed that if all music were to vanish except this collection, Western music could be reconstructed from it alone.

The Well-Tempered Clavier established the practical foundation of the Western tonal system and became the supreme textbook of harmony and counterpoint for every subsequent composer. Bach's fugues here range from two to five voices, in character from airy playfulness to solemn grandeur. Shostakovich, Hindemith, and Debussy all composed their own sets of preludes and fugues as deliberate homage to this inexhaustible monument.

famousScores.name: 《勃兰登堡协奏曲》 BWV 1046–1051
famousScores.desc: 六首协奏曲，巴洛克管弦乐的巅峰之作
famousScores.descEn: Six concertos representing the summit of Baroque orchestral writing
famousScores.detail:
《勃兰登堡协奏曲》创作于科滕宫廷时期（约1718-1721年），1721年3月巴赫将六首曲子整理成册，献给勃兰登堡-施韦特边侯克里斯蒂安·路德维希，扉页上用法语写着一封谦恭的求职信，希望获得边侯的赏识与保护。然而边侯似乎从未认真演奏过这些作品——直到他1734年去世，这些手稿以极低的价格被当作废纸出售。

这六首协奏曲每首采用不同的乐器组合，几乎是一部巴洛克乐器的百科全书：第一首使用两支狩猎圆号、第二首以小号为独奏，第三首仅用弦乐，第四首以两支竖笛和小提琴为独奏群，第五首以羽管键琴（大键琴）为主角——在历史上首次将键盘乐器提升为协奏曲的独奏主角，第六首则使用了古旧的维奥拉达甘巴（膝间提琴）。每首的风格和情绪截然不同，展示了巴赫无穷的创造力。

第五首协奏曲的第一乐章中，大键琴独奏部分包含了当时前所未见的长篇华彩段落（65小节），有学者认为这是巴赫展现自身钢琴技艺、向科滕宫廷证明自身价值的「现场展示」。《勃兰登堡协奏曲》对于后世协奏曲体裁的发展至关重要，其中多声部声部的精巧织体和主题发展手法直接影响了莫扎特、海顿乃至贝多芬的协奏曲写作。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Brandenburg Concertos were composed during Bach's years at the court of Cöthen (c. 1718–1721). In March 1721, he presented the six concertos to the Margrave of Brandenburg-Schwedt, Christian Ludwig, with a fawning cover letter in French seeking patronage. The Margrave apparently never had the works performed — when he died in 1734, the manuscripts were inventoried and sold for next to nothing as waste paper.

Each of the six concertos employs a different instrumental combination, making them collectively an encyclopedia of Baroque orchestration: the First uses two hunting horns; the Second features a high clarino trumpet; the Third is for strings alone; the Fourth casts two recorders alongside violin; the Fifth gives the harpsichord an unprecedented long cadenza (65 bars) as soloist — arguably the first time a keyboard instrument had taken center stage in a concerto; and the Sixth employs the archaic viola da gamba.

The extended harpsichord cadenza in the Fifth Concerto's first movement is thought by some scholars to be Bach's musical résumé — a dazzling display of his own keyboard virtuosity intended to secure his position at Cöthen. As models of scoring, thematic development, and formal architecture, the Brandenburgs profoundly influenced every concerto that came after, from Mozart and Haydn to Beethoven.

famousScores.name: 《无伴奏大提琴组曲》 BWV 1007–1012
famousScores.desc: 大提琴独奏文献的圣经，至今仍是每位大提琴家的必修曲目
famousScores.descEn: The bible of the solo cello repertoire, still essential study for every cellist
famousScores.detail:
六首《无伴奏大提琴组曲》创作于科滕宫廷时期（约1717-1723年），每首组曲均包含前奏曲、阿勒曼德、库朗特、萨拉班德、两首小舞曲（小步舞曲、布列舞曲或嘉禾舞曲）和基格。巴赫将当时被视为伴奏乐器的大提琴塑造成一件能够演奏多声部和声、承载完整音乐世界的独奏乐器，在技法上是一个革命性的突破。

这六首组曲在巴赫去世后几乎被彻底遗忘。1889年，13岁的巴勃罗·卡萨尔斯在巴塞罗那的一家旧书店里偶然发现了一册手稿副本——他的自传中记载这是他「一生中最大的发现」。卡萨尔斯此后花了12年时间研习这六首组曲，才在1902年公开演奏。他的历史录音（1936-1939年）至今仍是这套作品的权威诠释之一，他等于凭一己之力将这部作品从历史的尘埃中拯救了出来。

第三组曲前奏曲、第五组曲萨拉班德以及第六组曲的全曲被公认为大提琴文献中的最高峰。第六首是为一种被称为「五弦大提琴」的特殊乐器而作，使普通四弦大提琴演奏时面临极大挑战。这套组曲不仅是演奏技艺的试金石，更是无数音乐家心灵探索的指引——马友友曾六次重录，每次都带来截然不同的音乐理解。
famousScores.detailEn:
The six Cello Suites were composed during Bach's Cöthen years (c. 1717–1723). Each suite follows the pattern of Prelude, Allemande, Courante, Sarabande, two dance movements (minuets, bourrées, or gavottes), and Gigue. Bach transformed the cello — then regarded primarily as a continuo instrument — into a self-sufficient solo voice capable of implying harmony and sustaining an entire musical world: a revolutionary technical breakthrough.

After Bach's death, the suites fell into near-total obscurity. In 1889, the thirteen-year-old Pablo Casals stumbled upon a manuscript copy in a second-hand music shop in Barcelona — he later called it 'the greatest discovery of my life.' He spent twelve years studying the suites before daring to perform them publicly in 1902. His landmark recordings (1936–1939) remain authoritative interpretations to this day; Casals single-handedly rescued the works from oblivion.

The Prelude of Suite No. 3, the Sarabande of Suite No. 5, and the entirety of Suite No. 6 are universally acknowledged as the highest peaks of the cello literature. The Sixth Suite was written for a five-stringed instrument, making it a formidable challenge on the standard four-string cello. Yo-Yo Ma has re-recorded the complete set six times — each time revealing a wholly different musical understanding — testament to the suites' inexhaustible depth.

famousScores.name: 《b小调弥撒》 BWV 232
famousScores.desc: 巴赫毕生最宏大的声乐杰作，巴洛克合唱音乐的顶峰
famousScores.descEn: Bach's most monumental vocal achievement, the summit of Baroque choral music
famousScores.detail:
《b小调弥撒》的创作跨越了巴赫生命的最后25年，直到他去世前不久才最终完成。这部作品并非为单一场合所作：1733年巴赫将《基里埃》和《荣耀经》部分呈送给德累斯顿选帝侯，以求获得宫廷作曲家头衔；其余部分陆续完成，其中许多段落是从他早年的清唱剧中改编而来，体现了巴赫一贯的「自我借用」传统。整部弥撒长达两小时，远超任何实际礼拜的需要，是巴赫对天主教弥撒文本的理想化、纪念碑式的诠释。

这部作品有一个令人着迷的特点：巴赫本人是路德宗信徒，却将天主教弥撒文本谱成了如此宏大的作品。学者们认为，巴赫意在用这部弥撒为自己的对位与和声技艺作一次终极的总结与证明。其中「信经」（Credo）的中心段落「Et incarnatus est」（圣灵感孕而成人）仅用五个女声声部，以近乎神秘主义的静谧表现基督降临人世的神圣奥秘，是巴赫最令人心碎的音乐段落之一。

《b小调弥撒》与《马太受难曲》并称巴赫声乐作品的双峰，对后世的影响深远持久。贝多芬在创作《庄严弥撒》时曾细心研究这部作品；勃拉姆斯编辑出版了《b小调弥撒》的完整版本；卡尔·里克特1961年的录音被视为20世纪最伟大的指挥录音之一。这部弥撒证明，音乐可以在宗教仪式的边界之外追求一种纯粹精神性的崇高境界。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Mass in B minor took shape across the last twenty-five years of Bach's life and was only assembled into its final form shortly before his death. It was never composed as a unified work for a single occasion: in 1733 Bach submitted the Kyrie and Gloria to the Elector of Saxony in Dresden, seeking the title of court composer; the remaining movements were added over the following years, many of them reworked from earlier cantatas — a reflection of Bach's habitual 'self-borrowing.' At over two hours, the Mass far exceeds the requirements of any liturgy; it is Bach's idealized, monumental statement on the Latin Mass text.

One fascinating paradox: Bach was a devout Lutheran, yet he devoted his greatest effort to a Catholic Mass. Scholars believe he intended the work as a definitive, personal summing-up of his entire contrapuntal and harmonic art. The 'Et incarnatus est' at the heart of the Credo — scored for five female voices in a near-mystical hush — is among the most heartbreaking passages Bach ever wrote, conjuring the mystery of divine incarnation with barely a whisper.

Together with the St Matthew Passion, the B minor Mass stands as the twin summit of Bach's vocal output. Beethoven studied it carefully while composing his Missa Solemnis; Brahms edited and published the complete score. Karl Richter's 1961 recording is regarded as one of the greatest conducting achievements of the twentieth century. The Mass demonstrates that music can pursue a purely spiritual sublimity entirely beyond the bounds of liturgical function.

famousScores.name: 《d小调托卡塔与赋格》 BWV 565
famousScores.nameEn: Toccata and Fugue in D minor, BWV 565
famousScores.desc: 最广为人知的管风琴作品，开篇音型震撼人心
famousScores.descEn: The most universally recognized organ work, with an opening gesture of electrifying power
famousScores.detail:
《d小调托卡塔与赋格》BWV 565大约创作于巴赫青年时代（1703-1707年），当时他正在魏玛和阿恩施塔特担任管风琴师，深受北德管风琴学派大师布克斯特胡德的影响。作品以一个孤独的单声部下行音型开始，随即爆发为震撼人心的满声部和弦，这一开头已成为西方音乐史上辨识度最高的音乐形象之一。托卡塔部分炫技性极强，自由奔放；赋格部分则以简洁的主题展开精密的三声部对位。

这部作品有一段离奇的「版权」故事：20世纪初，德国学者彼得·威廉斯提出了一个惊人假说——BWV 565可能根本不是巴赫所作，而是某位后人将一首小提琴或大提琴作品改编为管风琴曲后伪托巴赫之名。他的理由是：作品风格与巴赫成熟期大相径庭，开篇的单声部写法也不像管风琴惯例。这一争论至今悬而未决，但绝大多数音乐学家仍倾向于认为它是真品。

1940年，迪士尼将这首作品用于动画电影《幻想曲》的开场，由利奥波德·斯托科夫斯基指挥管弦乐改编版演奏。这部电影使《d小调托卡塔与赋格》成为了整个20世纪流行文化中古典音乐的符号，出现在无数电影、电视、广告、摇滚乐改编和电子游戏中。这种超越时代的流行性本身便是奇迹——一个17世纪的管风琴手可曾想到，他的即兴演奏有朝一日会响彻全世界？
famousScores.detailEn:
The Toccata and Fugue in D minor BWV 565 is thought to date from Bach's youth (c. 1703–1707), when he was serving as an organist in Weimar and Arnstadt, absorbing the influence of the North German master Buxtehude. It begins with a solitary descending single-line figure that explodes into a full-voiced chord of electrifying impact — one of the most instantly recognizable openings in all of Western music. The Toccata section is brilliantly improvisatory; the Fugue builds a tight three-voice web from a crisp, angular subject.

The work has a peculiar attribution controversy: early in the 20th century, the German scholar Peter Williams proposed the startling hypothesis that BWV 565 might not be by Bach at all, but rather a later arrangement of a violin or cello piece falsely credited to him — citing its unconventionally thin opening texture and stylistic anomalies. The debate remains technically unresolved, though the overwhelming consensus among musicologists still accepts the attribution.

In 1940, Disney used the piece — in Stokowski's lavish orchestral transcription — as the opening number of the animated film Fantasia. The film transformed the Toccata and Fugue into the defining symbol of classical music in popular culture throughout the twentieth century, appearing in countless films, television programs, advertisements, rock arrangements, and video games. That a Baroque organ improvisation should become a global icon of 'classical music' is itself a kind of miracle no 17th-century church organist could have imagined.

famousScores.name: 《赋格的艺术》 BWV 1080
famousScores.desc: 巴赫的天鹅之歌，对位艺术的终极探索，未竟而终
famousScores.descEn: Bach's swan song — the ultimate exploration of counterpoint, left unfinished at his death
famousScores.detail:
《赋格的艺术》是巴赫在生命最后几年（约1742-1749年）精心构筑的对位艺术总结。全集以一个简单的d小调主题为核心，将其以各种对位手法逐步变形：镜像、逆行、增值、减值、双重与三重赋格……每一首「赋格曲」（Contrapunctus）和卡农都是对同一主题的全新透视，共同构成一部庞大的对位宇宙。巴赫在生前显然有意将此作为自己创作生涯的终极总结。

最著名的未完成之谜发生在最后一首三重赋格（Contrapunctus XIV）中：就在赋格展开第三个主题的时候，乐谱戛然中断。后人在手稿上发现，第三个主题是B—A—C—H的音名（德文音名体系中，B=降B，A=A，C=C，H=B）。这意味着巴赫将自己的名字隐藏在了这部绝笔之作里，仿佛一个未竟的自画像。研究者们争论了两个世纪：他究竟是在失明与病痛中放弃了，还是另有更多完成的部分遗失了？

《赋格的艺术》是音乐史上最具哲学意味的作品之一，它超越了乐器的具体指定（手稿上并无乐器标注）而成为纯粹的「音乐思想」。这种抽象性使20世纪的数学家和哲学家也对它着迷——侯世达在其名著《哥德尔、埃舍尔、巴赫》中将它与数理逻辑和视觉悖论并列讨论，认为巴赫在音乐中触及了人类思维的本质结构。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Art of Fugue was assembled during the final years of Bach's life (c. 1742–1749) as his ultimate summation of contrapuntal art. All of its movements — titled Contrapunctus — are built from a single D minor theme, subjected to every imaginable transformation: inversion, retrograde, augmentation, diminution, double and triple fugue. Each Contrapunctus and canon reveals an entirely new aspect of the same kernel of material, together forming a vast contrapuntal cosmos.

The work's most haunting mystery lies in its final triple fugue, Contrapunctus XIV: the score breaks off abruptly just as the third theme enters — and that theme spells the letters B–A–C–H in German musical notation (B♭–A–C–B♮). Bach had embedded his own name as a hidden voice in his final testament. Scholars have debated for two centuries whether he abandoned the work through illness and failing sight, or whether further completed pages were simply lost.

The Art of Fugue is one of the most philosophically resonant works in music history. Bach left no instrumentation markings, making it pure musical thought, independent of any specific medium. This abstraction has fascinated mathematicians and philosophers as well as musicians: Douglas Hofstadter's celebrated book Gödel, Escher, Bach places it alongside mathematical logic and visual paradox, arguing that Bach touched the essential structures of human thought itself.

famousScores.name: 《恰空》 d小调，出自《小提琴帕蒂塔》第二首 BWV 1004
famousScores.nameEn: Chaconne in D minor, from Violin Partita No. 2, BWV 1004
famousScores.desc: 单把小提琴演奏的宏篇史诗，被誉为「人类音乐中最伟大的单一乐章」
famousScores.descEn: An epic for solo violin alone, called 'the greatest single movement ever composed for a solo instrument'
famousScores.detail:
《d小调恰空》是《无伴奏小提琴帕蒂塔第二首》的最终乐章，全曲演奏时间约15分钟，是帕蒂塔前四个乐章长度之和——它本身就几乎是一部独立的交响诗。恰空（Chaconne）是一种建立在持续低音进行之上的变奏曲式，巴赫以这一简单的四小节低音框架为支撑，构筑了一座由65个变奏组成的音乐大教堂，从悲剧性的d小调经过光明的D大调再回到黑暗的d小调，构成一个命运般的轮回。

有学者提出了一个极具说服力的解读：1720年，当巴赫从漫长的宫廷出巡回到科滕时，发现他深爱的妻子玛丽亚·芭芭拉已猝然离世。这首《恰空》创作于同年，有研究者认为整部帕蒂塔——尤其是这首《恰空》——是对亡妻的悼念。中间大调部分的圣咏旋律「Wie wohl ist mir, o Freund der Seelen」（我的灵魂是多么幸福）更被认为是对亡妻的深情追忆。尽管无法证实，但这一解读赋予了音乐难以言说的情感重量。

约翰尼斯·勃拉姆斯在给克拉拉·舒曼的信中写道：「如果我能想象如何创作这首曲子，我会因为过度兴奋和震撼而当场昏厥。」他后来将其改编为钢琴左手独奏版。布索尼的钢琴改编版、谢格尔的管弦乐版以及海菲兹、帕尔曼、希拉里·哈恩的小提琴录音，都是20世纪音乐表演史上的丰碑。这首《恰空》被菲利普·格拉斯评为「人类历史上最重要的音乐作品」。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Chaconne is the final movement of the Partita No. 2 in D minor for solo violin, lasting some fifteen minutes — longer than the four preceding movements combined, and virtually a self-contained symphonic poem. Built on a repeating four-bar bass progression, Bach erects a vast architecture of sixty-five variations, moving from tragic D minor through a luminous D major episode and back into darkness, tracing a fateful arc of loss and transcendence.

A compelling and moving hypothesis has been proposed: in 1720, Bach returned from a long court journey to find that his beloved first wife, Maria Barbara, had died suddenly in his absence. The Partita was composed that same year. Scholars argue that the entire work — and this Chaconne above all — is an act of mourning for her. The chorale melody that appears in the central major section, 'Wie wohl ist mir, o Freund der Seelen' (How blessed is my soul), has been identified as a direct quotation of a funeral hymn. Though unprovable, this reading gives the music an almost unbearable emotional weight.

Brahms wrote to Clara Schumann: 'If I could picture myself writing, or even conceiving, the piece, I am quite certain that the extreme excitement and emotional tension would have driven me mad.' He later arranged it for solo piano left hand. Busoni's piano transcription, Stokowski's orchestration, and recordings by Heifetz, Perlman, and Hilary Hahn stand as monuments of 20th-century performance. Philip Glass has named it 'the most important piece of music ever composed.'

famousScores.name: 《我已知足》清唱剧 BWV 82
famousScores.desc: 巴赫最深情的独唱清唱剧，对死亡的平静接受令人动容
famousScores.descEn: Bach's most tender solo cantata — a serene, deeply moving meditation on accepting death
famousScores.detail:
《我已知足》（Ich habe genug）BWV 82创作于1727年，是巴赫为圣烛节（2月2日）而作的独唱清唱剧。全曲包含三首咏叹调和两首宣叙调，以男低音独唱为主角，主题是西面老人（Simeon）抱着圣婴耶稣后说出的那句话：「主啊，如今你可以照你的话，释放你的仆人安然去世。」整部作品弥漫着一种对死亡的平静渴望——不是恐惧，而是对甜蜜解脱的期待。

最令音乐家们着迷的是第二首咏叹调「沉睡吧」（Schlummert ein）：长笛以摇篮曲般轻柔的旋律催眠，男低音平静地唱出「我闭上了眼睛，快乐地沉睡」。这段音乐是巴赫对「死亡就是甜蜜的睡眠」这一路德宗神学观念最完美的音乐诠释。有人说，这首咏叹调是巴赫写给自己的一首安魂曲——他在晚年反复修订这部清唱剧，显然对其格外珍视。

指挥家约翰·埃利奥特·加德纳曾将BWV 82列为巴赫最重要的五部清唱剧之一。20世纪中期，德裔男低音迪特里希·费舍尔-迪斯考的录音使这首曲子广为人知；此后，英国男中音胡苏·普雷加迪安的本真演奏版本又为它带来了全新的生命。这部清唱剧以其内敛的深情和对死亡的坦然态度，成为许多人在生命最艰难时刻的精神慰藉。
famousScores.detailEn:
Cantata BWV 82, 'Ich habe genug' ('I am content'), was composed in 1727 for the Feast of the Purification (February 2nd). It is a solo cantata for bass voice, comprising three arias and two recitatives, meditating on the words of the aged Simeon who, having held the infant Jesus, says: 'Lord, now lettest thou thy servant depart in peace.' The entire work is suffused with a serene longing for death — not dread, but a gentle anticipation of sweet release.

The second aria, 'Schlummert ein' (Slumber now), is among the most quietly spellbinding passages Bach ever wrote. The flute spins a lullaby-like melody while the bass voice sings peacefully, 'I close my eyes, I fall happily asleep.' This is Bach's most perfect musical expression of the Lutheran theology of death as blessed sleep — a belief he clearly held deeply. The cantata was revised several times in Bach's later years, suggesting a personal attachment to its message.

Conductor John Eliot Gardiner has placed BWV 82 among the five most important Bach cantatas. Dietrich Fischer-Dieskau's mid-century recordings brought it wide attention; the countertenor Andreas Scholl's and baritone Peter Kooy's period-instrument performances gave it entirely new life in the 21st century. This cantata has become, for many listeners, a source of spiritual consolation at the most difficult moments of life — a quality that transcends all confessional boundaries.

influence: 巴赫对后世的影响几乎无法估量。莫扎特研究巴赫后深受启发，贝多芬称他为「和声之父」。在浪漫主义时期，门德尔松、舒曼等人重新发掘了他的作品。进入20世纪，勋伯格的十二音体系可以追溯到巴赫对位法的逻辑延伸。爵士乐、电子音乐甚至摇滚乐中都能找到巴赫的影子。他不仅是巴洛克的集大成者，更是整个西方音乐的根基。
influenceEn: Bach's influence on posterity is virtually immeasurable. Mozart was profoundly inspired after studying Bach's works; Beethoven called him the 'father of harmony.' During the Romantic era, Mendelssohn, Schumann, and others championed his rediscovery. In the 20th century, Schoenberg's twelve-tone system can be traced back as a logical extension of Bachian counterpoint. Echoes of Bach resound in jazz, electronic music, and even rock. He stands not merely as the supreme master of the Baroque, but as the very bedrock of Western music.
